The RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) and CISC (Complex Instruction Set Computer) architectures represent contrasting design philosophies rooted in different eras of computing. RISC architectures like MIPS, SPARC, and ARM emphasize simplicity and efficiency. Their instruction sets are streamlined, with a focus on orthogonal, general-purpose instructions that can be executed quickly in a single cycle. This philosophy was born from the realization that hardware complexity could be traded for software simplicity
